source.title: Spatial contextual recognition memory updating is modulated by dopamine release in the dorsal hippocampus from the locus coeruleus.
source.venue: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

confidence_rationale: Direct within-study dissociation of LC-CA1 vs VTA-CA1 terminals in memory updating, with microdialysis confirmation that LC inhibition (not VTA) reduces dorsal HPC dopamine and noradrenaline, plus LTP suppression as mechanistic link. Strengthens the dorsal=LC arm of MECH-075 and shows gain effects translate to plasticity threshold (LTP induction). Evidence direction: supports the LC-mediated dorsal gain arm specifically.
failure_signatures: Photoinhibition of LC-CA1 (not VTA-CA1) terminals impairs spatial contextual memory updating
LC-CA1 inhibition reduces both dopamine and noradrenaline in dorsal CA1 by microdialysis
D1/D5 antagonism but not beta-adrenergic antagonism impairs memory updating behavior
LC-CA1 inhibition suppresses LTP induction in Schaffer collaterals
VTA-CA1 inhibition has no effect on dorsal HPC catecholamines or memory updating

mapping.source_claim_statement: Memory updating in dorsal HPC requires catecholamine release from LC-CA1 terminals; this operates via D1/D5 receptors and suppresses LTP when blocked; VTA-CA1 terminals are not involved.
mapping.ree_translation: The LC-mediated gain arm of MECH-075 operates specifically on memory update/write events in the dorsal HPC (E3 viability map). When a stored viability map entry is retrieved and must be updated, LC activation sets the threshold for LTP induction. This is the gain-setting mechanism that MECH-075 claims BG/dopaminergic tone provides -- here shown directly for the LC->dorsal HPC arm. The D1/D5 specificity (not beta-adrenergic) isolates dopamine co-release as the active signal.
mapping.mapping_caveat: This is a memory reconsolidation/updating paradigm, not a first-encoding paradigm. REE viability map updates (write events after novel outcome) may map onto the reconsolidation context; initial map formation may differ. Also, LTP threshold modulation is inferred from suppression experiments; attractor basin geometry not directly measured.
mapping.source_context: Mouse, dorsal CA1, spatial object location memory updating, optogenetic terminal inhibition, in vivo microdialysis

confidence_components.notes: Highly controlled optogenetic dissociation with microdialysis ground truth -- strong source quality. Mapping fidelity: the reconsolidation/updating frame fits REE write-gating well, but the specific attractor dynamics (basin stability, competitive inhibition) are not directly measured. Transfer risk moderate: mouse spatial memory to REE viability map is conceptually tight but computationally unvalidated.
